editor_settings.title: Is Fujifilm X100VI Still Worth Buying in 2026?
editor_settings.content:
Is Fujifilm X100VI Still Worth Buying in 2026?

The Fujifilm X100VI is still one of the coolest cameras you can buy.

It is also still one of the easiest cameras to overpay for.

That is the whole story in one sentence.

Quick Verdict

Yes, the X100VI is still worth buying in 2026 if you want a premium everyday camera with Fujifilm color, great straight-out-of-camera JPEGs, and a fixed 35mm equivalent look you already know you love.

No, it is not the smartest buy if you mainly care about value, flexibility, or pure specs per dollar. As of May 3, 2026, Adorama was still showing the X100VI at $1,799 and on backorder, which tells you the hype has not cooled off much.

What You Are Actually Paying For

You are not just paying for a sensor and a lens.

You are paying for a camera that feels special in the hand, travels light, looks good enough that people actually carry it, and makes JPEG shooting feel fun again. That matters more than a lot of online spec wars admit.

Fujifilm backs that up with real hardware too. The X100VI gives you a 40.2MP APS-C sensor, a fixed 23mm f/2 lens, up to 6 stops of in-body image stabilization, a built-in ND filter, 20 film simulation modes, and 6.2K video. That is a serious camera, not just a fashion object.

Why People Still Keep Chasing It

The appeal is simple. It is one of the rare cameras that feels both practical and emotional.

You can use it as a travel camera, a street camera, a family camera, a memory camera, or just a daily carry that makes ordinary life look a little better. And because the JPEGs are often already pretty, it reduces the editing burden that burns a lot of people out.

Where the Buying Decision Gets Ugly

The price is the problem, not the camera.

At normal retail, the X100VI is already expensive for a fixed-lens APS-C camera. Add backorders, impatient buyers, and scarcity-driven pricing, and the whole thing starts feeling a little absurd.

If you are buying just because social media made it look rare and magical, slow down.

If you are buying because you already know this exact fixed-lens, film-sim-heavy, everyday-carry style fits how you shoot, then the answer gets much easier.

Who Should Buy It

  • people who love the 35mm equivalent field of view
  • travel shooters who want one compact camera instead of a whole kit
  • Fujifilm fans who want strong JPEGs and fast sharing through XApp
  • creators who care as much about shooting experience as technical quality

Who Should Skip It

  • buyers who want interchangeable lenses
  • people who need a zoom
  • anyone mainly chasing value
  • people who are unsure whether they even like a fixed 35mm equivalent camera

If you want the X100VI without the full new-stock pain, a used bundle like this is the much easier buy to defend:

[product-card:fujifilm-x100vi-silver-camera-used-with-accessories-premium-compact-camera]


That kind of pricing makes the camera feel exciting again instead of annoying.

Final Verdict

The Fujifilm X100VI is still worth buying in 2026.

It is just not worth buying blindly.

If you genuinely want this camera for what it is, the appeal is real. If you just want the internet's favorite camera, save your money.

editor_settings.meta_description: The Fujifilm X100VI is still one of the most wanted compact cameras in 2026. But is it actually worth buying at current prices and stock conditions?
